slides.title: Comparing Sets
slides.text: We can compare Sets to see how they relate. Are they the same? Do they share members?

slides.title: 1. Equal Sets (=)
slides.text:
Sets are Equal if they have exactly the same members.

A = {1, 2, 3}
B = {3, 1, 2}

Even if the order is different, they are the same set!

slides.title: 2. Equivalent Sets (↔)
slides.text:
Sets are Equivalent if they have the same number of members (same cardinality).

n(A) = 3 and n(B) = 3.
They don't need to have the same items.

slides.title: 3. Disjoint Sets
slides.text:
Disjoint Sets have nothing in common. Their intersection is empty (Ø).

Example: Cats and Dogs. No animal is both!

slides.title: Review
slides.text:
• Equal: Same members inside.
  • Equivalent: Same amount (count).
  • Disjoint: No overlap.
